content.full_text:
July 30, 1990

Mary Ann Kvasager
Technical Assistance and Analysis Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
428 J Street
Sacramento, CA 95814-0814

Dear Mary Ann:

This will confirm our telephone conversation on July 25, 1990. At that time you provided advice and assistance to me with respect to a question that I had previously submitted to you. This will summarize the question and advice given.

I described a situation involving an association which has established a separate bank account for political expenditures and registered with the California Secretary of State's office as a recipient committee. The primary purpose of the registered account is to oppose a particular statewide ballot measure in California. In this regard, the association directly and through its registered account has also provided support to another separate registered recipient committee in California, whose purpose is to oppose the same statewide ballot measure.

I indicated that the association's registered account has made direct contributions to the separate committee. In addition, the association has made certain non-monetary contributions to the separate recipient committee to oppose the particular statewide ballot measure. There has been an unity of purpose between the association and the association's registered account, both of which entities are controlled by the same persons.

I asked you for guidance as to the manner in which these transactions could be reported, including if the association's non-monetary contributions could be reported on the association's registered account Form 420.

After checking with Jeanne Pritchard and others in your office, you advised me that the association could include its non-monetary contributions as a memo entry on the allocation page of the association's registered account Form 420. I

Mary Ann Kvasager  
July 30, 1990  
Page Two

confirmed to you that the separate registered recipient committee would be reporting these non-monetary contributions on its Schedule C. I also asked if the association's separate registered account report could include the non-monetary contributions of the association on Schedule C of its report as well, but you said that this should not be done because it would involve double reporting. You emphasized that the reporting of the non-monetary contributions on the allocation page would be sufficient as the cleanest and simplest way to report these transactions according to the persons in your office with whom you checked.

Thank you very much for your advice. We will proceed in accordance with your guidance.

Very truly yours,

[handwritten signature]
Peter A. Bagatilos

PAB/mlq
tcs:7-30mk
